Package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean

Class BeanConfigContext

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanConfigContext
public final class BeanConfigContext extends Object
Immutable runtime configuration for the bean-modeling layer.

BeanConfigContext is the runtime sibling of the @BeanConfig annotation: it carries the resolved values that drive how Java types are introspected as beans, independent of any marshalling concern. Settings include visibility thresholds, required-property toggles, fluent-setter detection, property naming, not-bean exclusions, the active BeanStore and AnnotationProvider, and a few related hooks.

This type is the bean-modeling counterpart of the marshalling-layer context that lives in juneau-marshall. It exists so the bean-modeling runtime (in commons.bean) can be used independently of the marshalling stack, and so the marshalling layer can compose a BeanConfigContext snapshot to feed bean-modeling code without exposing marshalling-only APIs.

Construction:

// All defaults. BeanConfigContext defaults = BeanConfigContext.DEFAULT; // Customized. BeanConfigContext ctx = BeanConfigContext.create() .beanClassVisibility(Visibility.PROTECTED) .findFluentSetters(true) .propertyNamer(new PropertyNamerDLC()) .build(); // Copy and tweak. BeanConfigContext ctx2 = ctx.copy() .unsortedProperties(true) .build();

Thread safety
BeanConfigContext instances are immutable and thread-safe. BeanConfigContext.Builder instances are mutable and not thread-safe.
See Also:
  • @BeanConfig — the annotation form.

    Returns the BeanPropertyPostProcessor SPI hook used during BeanPropertyMeta construction.
    BeanStore
    getBeanStore()
    Returns the active BeanStore used for factory-based bean instantiation, or null if none configured.
    String
    getBeanTypePropertyName()
    Returns the property name used to embed the bean dictionary type name (default: "_type").

    Returns the set of classes (and supertypes) that are explicitly excluded from bean detection.

    getNotBeanPackageNames()
    Returns the set of fully qualified package names whose classes are excluded from bean detection.

    getNotBeanPackagePrefixes()
    Returns the set of fully qualified package prefixes whose classes are excluded from bean detection.

    getPropertyNamer()
    Returns the PropertyNamer used to derive property names from getter/setter/field names.
    boolean
    isBeanMapPutReturnsOldValue()
    Returns whether BeanMap.put(String,Object) returns the previous property value.
    boolean
    isBeansRequireDefaultConstructor()
    Returns whether classes must have a no-arg constructor to be considered beans.

    isBeansRequireSerializable()
    Returns whether classes must implement Serializable to be considered beans.
    boolean
    isBeansRequireSettersForGetters()
    Returns whether bean properties must have a setter to be considered writable from the getter.
    boolean
    isBeansRequireSomeProperties()
    Returns whether classes must have at least one property to be considered beans.
    boolean
    isFindFluentSetters()
    Returns whether fluent-style setters (returning this) should be detected.
    boolean
    isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()
    Returns whether exceptions thrown from bean property getters should be silently swallowed.

    Returns whether exceptions thrown from bean property setters should be silently swallowed.

    Returns whether bean properties without setters should be silently ignored during deserialization.

    boolean

    Returns whether attempts to set unknown bean properties to null should be silently ignored.

    Returns whether properties should preserve their JVM-discovered (non-alphabetical) order.
    boolean
    isUseInterfaceProxies()
    Returns whether interface proxies should be created for bean interfaces.
    boolean

    Returns whether Introspector should be used to discover bean properties.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT

      public static final BeanConfigContext DEFAULT
      Default BeanConfigContext instance with all settings at their defaults.

      Equivalent to BeanConfigContext.create().build().

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static BeanConfigContext.Builder create()
      Creates a new builder for BeanConfigContext.
      Returns:
      A new builder.

    • copy

      public BeanConfigContext.Builder copy()
      Returns a builder pre-populated with the values from this context.
      Returns:
      A new builder.

    • getBeanClassVisibility

      public Visibility getBeanClassVisibility()
      Minimum bean class visibility.

      Classes are not considered beans unless they meet this minimum visibility requirement.

      Returns:
      The minimum bean class visibility. Never null.

    • getBeanConstructorVisibility

      public Visibility getBeanConstructorVisibility()
      Minimum bean constructor visibility.
      Returns:
      The minimum bean constructor visibility. Never null.

    • getBeanFieldVisibility

      public Visibility getBeanFieldVisibility()
      Minimum bean field visibility.
      Returns:
      The minimum bean field visibility. Never null.

    • getBeanMethodVisibility

      public Visibility getBeanMethodVisibility()
      Minimum bean method visibility.
      Returns:
      The minimum bean method visibility. Never null.

    • isBeanMapPutReturnsOldValue

      public boolean isBeanMapPutReturnsOldValue()
      Returns whether BeanMap.put(String,Object) returns the previous property value.

      When false (the default), BeanMap.put(...) always returns null for performance reasons — the underlying bean's getter is skipped before the setter is invoked.

      Returns:
      true if old values are returned.

    • isBeansRequireDefaultConstructor

      public boolean isBeansRequireDefaultConstructor()
      Returns whether classes must have a no-arg constructor to be considered beans.
      Returns:
      true if a no-arg constructor is required.

    • isBeansRequireSerializable

      public boolean isBeansRequireSerializable()
      Returns whether classes must implement Serializable to be considered beans.
      Returns:
      true if Serializable is required.

    • isBeansRequireSettersForGetters

      public boolean isBeansRequireSettersForGetters()
      Returns whether bean properties must have a setter to be considered writable from the getter.
      Returns:
      true if setters are required for getters.

    • isBeansRequireSomeProperties

      public boolean isBeansRequireSomeProperties()
      Returns whether classes must have at least one property to be considered beans.
      Returns:
      true if at least one property is required.

    • isFindFluentSetters

      public boolean isFindFluentSetters()
      Returns whether fluent-style setters (returning this) should be detected.
      Returns:
      true if fluent setters are detected.

    • isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters

      public boolean isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()
      Returns whether exceptions thrown from bean property getters should be silently swallowed.

      When true, an exception thrown by a getter is treated as a null property value rather than propagated to the caller.

      Returns:
      true if getter invocation exceptions are ignored.

    • isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters

      public boolean isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()
      Returns whether exceptions thrown from bean property setters should be silently swallowed.

      When true, an exception thrown by a setter is suppressed rather than propagated to the caller.

      Returns:
      true if setter invocation exceptions are ignored.

    • isIgnoreMissingSetters

      public boolean isIgnoreMissingSetters()
      Returns whether bean properties without setters should be silently ignored during deserialization.
      Returns:
      true if missing setters are ignored.

    • isIgnoreTransientFields

      public boolean isIgnoreTransientFields()
      Returns whether transient fields should be excluded from bean property detection.
      Returns:
      true if transient fields are ignored.

    • isIgnoreUnknownBeanProperties

      public boolean isIgnoreUnknownBeanProperties()
      Returns whether unknown properties on incoming bean payloads should be ignored.
      Returns:
      true if unknown properties are ignored.

    • isIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties

      public boolean isIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()
      Returns whether attempts to set unknown bean properties to null should be silently ignored.

      When true (the default), trying to set an unknown property to null is a no-op rather than an error. When false, the property setter still rejects unknown property names.

      Returns:
      true if unknown null properties are silently ignored.

    • isUnsortedProperties

      public boolean isUnsortedProperties()
      Returns whether properties should preserve their JVM-discovered (non-alphabetical) order.
      Returns:
      true if properties remain unsorted.

    • isUseInterfaceProxies

      public boolean isUseInterfaceProxies()
      Returns whether interface proxies should be created for bean interfaces.
      Returns:
      true if interface proxies are enabled.

    • isUseJavaBeanIntrospector

      public boolean isUseJavaBeanIntrospector()
      Returns whether Introspector should be used to discover bean properties.
      Returns:
      true if the JavaBeans introspector is used.

    • getPropertyNamer

      public PropertyNamer getPropertyNamer()
      Returns the PropertyNamer used to derive property names from getter/setter/field names.
      Returns:
      The active property namer. Never null.

    • getBeanTypePropertyName

      public String getBeanTypePropertyName()
      Returns the property name used to embed the bean dictionary type name (default: "_type").
      Returns:
      The bean type property name. Never null.

    • getNotBeanPackageNames

      public Set<String> getNotBeanPackageNames()
      Returns the set of fully qualified package names whose classes are excluded from bean detection.
      Returns:
      The not-a-bean package name set. Never null. Unmodifiable.

    • getNotBeanPackagePrefixes

      public Set<String> getNotBeanPackagePrefixes()
      Returns the set of fully qualified package prefixes whose classes are excluded from bean detection.
      Returns:
      The not-a-bean package prefix set. Never null. Unmodifiable.

    • getNotBeanClasses

      public Set<Class<?>> getNotBeanClasses()
      Returns the set of classes (and supertypes) that are explicitly excluded from bean detection.
      Returns:
      The not-a-bean class set. Never null. Unmodifiable.

    • getBeanStore

      public BeanStore getBeanStore()
      Returns the active BeanStore used for factory-based bean instantiation, or null if none configured.
      Returns:
      The bean store, or null.

    • getAnnotationProvider

      public AnnotationProvider getAnnotationProvider()
      Returns the AnnotationProvider used to discover annotations on classes/methods/fields/parameters.
      Returns:
      The annotation provider. Never null.

    • getBeanMetaInitializer

      public BeanMetaInitializer getBeanMetaInitializer()
      Returns the BeanMetaInitializer SPI hook used during BeanMeta construction.

      On the commons-side path this is BeanMetaInitializer.NOOP (no marshalling-side help); on the marshalling-side path, MarshallingContext wires its own implementation that reads @Marshalled / @BeanType annotations and builds BeanRegistry instances.

      Returns:
      The bean-meta initializer. Never null.

    • getBeanPropertyPostProcessor

      public BeanPropertyPostProcessor getBeanPropertyPostProcessor()
      Returns the BeanPropertyPostProcessor SPI hook used during BeanPropertyMeta construction.

      On the commons-side path this is BeanPropertyPostProcessor.NOOP (no swap-aware transforms or @MarshalledProp / @Swap / @Uri reads); on the marshalling-side path, MarshallingContext wires its own implementation.

      Returns:
      The bean-property post-processor. Never null.

    • isNotABean

      public boolean isNotABean(ClassInfo ci)
      Returns true if the specified class is excluded from bean detection.

      The default implementation checks getNotBeanClasses(), getNotBeanPackageNames(), and getNotBeanPackagePrefixes(), and rejects arrays/primitives/enums/annotations. A custom predicate (set via BeanConfigContext.Builder.notABeanPredicate(Predicate)) overrides this behavior entirely.

      Parameters:
      ci - The class info being tested. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      true if the class is excluded from bean detection.
